title.en: Protecting video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony traffic

group-parts.group: Protecting video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony traffic
group-parts.name: overview

prose.en: Video conferencing and IP telephony traffic can be vulnerable to eavesdropping, denial-of-service, person-in-the-middle and call spoofing attacks. To mitigate this security risk, video conferencing and IP telephony signalling and audio/video data can be protected with the use of Transport Layer Security. This is achieved by using the Session Initiation Protocol Secure protocol and the Secure Real-time Transport Protocol.
